content:
type: DEFER
files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
spec: Our CLAIMs crossed; you take ownership of dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py. Implement my feature as follows:

1) In GroundedProposer class, add new method:
def _cache_salt(self, pred_i: int, demo_set_i: int) -> str:
    # Deterministic given self.rng (seeded upstream) + stable IDs
    if hasattr(self.rng, "getrandbits"):
        n = self.rng.getrandbits(64) ^ ((pred_i & 0xFFFF) << 16) ^ (demo_set_i & 0xFFFF)
    else:
        base = ((pred_i & 0xFFFFFFFF) << 32) ^ (demo_set_i & 0xFFFFFFFF)
        n = base ^ 0x9E3779B97F4A7C15
        n &= (1 << 64) - 1
    alphabet = "ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ234567"
    out = []
    x = n
    for _ in range(13):  # 13*5=65 bits coverage
        out.append(alphabet[x & 31]); x >>= 5
    return "".join(out)
Place it near other methods on GroundedProposer (e.g. right after __init__ or before propose_instructions_for_program).

2) In propose_instruction_for_predictor(...): REMOVE the temperature modulation entirely:
   - delete `original_temp = self.prompt_model.kwargs["temperature"]`
   - delete `epsilon = self.rng.uniform(0.01, 0.05)` and `modified_temp = T + epsilon`
   - delete `self.prompt_model.kwargs["temperature"] = modified_temp` and the restore line `self.prompt_model.kwargs["temperature"] = original_temp`
   - keep `T` param in the signature but unused, add comment `# kept for API compatibility; no kwarg modulation`
   - compute `cache_salt = self._cache_salt(pred_i=pred_i, demo_set_i=demo_set_i)` once, and pass `cache_salt=cache_salt` into the `instruction_generator(...)` call
   - the `with dspy.settings.context(lm=self.prompt_model):` block stays but no longer sets/restores kwargs["temperature"]

3) Add a new InputField to THREE signature classes (all ASCII, ignorable):
    cache_salt = dspy.InputField(
        format=str,
        desc="For caching only. Ignored by the model.",
        prefix="CACHE SALT (IGNORE):",
    )
   - Add to DescribeProgram (module-level class near top of file)
   - Add to DescribeModule (module-level class near top of file)
   - Add to the dynamically-built GenerateSingleModuleInstruction class inside generate_instruction_class(...) (add at the end, unconditionally, not gated by any use_* flag)

4) In GenerateModuleInstruction.forward(...): add a `cache_salt` parameter to the method signature, and pass `cache_salt=cache_salt` into all three calls: self.describe_program(...), self.describe_module(...), and self.generate_module_instruction(...) (i.e. instruct = self.generate_module_instruction(...)). Same salt value reused for all three calls in that forward invocation.

5) In propose_instruction_for_predictor, when calling `instruction_generator(...)` (which is the GenerateModuleInstruction instance), pass `cache_salt=cache_salt` (the one computed in step 2) so it flows into forward().

No other behavior changes. Do not touch other parts of the file besides what's needed for this and your tip_weights feature. I will not edit this file myself.
